summary: Session Notes (cross-session memory) feature fully tested and verified. All 25 tests pass. Feature works end-to-end: after exactly 6 user messages, asyncio background task calls OpenRouter to summarise the session and persists notes to users.session_notes. GET /api/chat/notes and DELETE /api/chat/notes endpoints work correctly. All regression tests pass (auth/me, chat history).

minor.issue: Response wraps user data in a 'user' key: {"user": {...}} instead of flat object. Inconsistent with some frontend expectations — worth documenting. Not a bug, just an API design note.

critical_code_review_comments: update_session_notes uses asyncio.create_task() — requires a running event loop. Works correctly in FastAPI async context. ✓

Session notes trigger fires every 6 user messages via chat_histories in-memory count. If server restarts mid-session, count resets to 0 and trigger may not fire until next 6 messages — this is by design but worth noting.
Notes are capped at 1500 chars (new_notes.strip()[:1500]) — tested and confirmed working.
load_personal_context() uses try-except and returns '' on error — safe failure path tested.
session_notes_updated_at stored as isoformat string (not datetime object) — consistent with other timestamp fields in the DB.

update_session_notes passes req.session_id (without user_id prefix) to DB query, while chat_histories uses f'{user_id}_{req.session_id}' as key — this is correct and intentional, both query the same DB session_id.

test_credentials.guest: Auto-created via POST /api/auth/guest (used for 6-message trigger test)

seed_data_creation: Fresh guest users created for 6-message trigger test (isolated, non-destructive). test@test.com notes were NOT modified — test used guest user for isolation.

mocked_apis.openrouter: REAL — OpenRouter API called with live key. Notes generation returned actual Russian-language psychological session summary.
mocked_apis.stripe_webhooks: MOCKED — not tested in this iteration

context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 10 verified Session Notes feature. All 25 tests pass. Key findings: (1) GET /api/chat/notes returns {notes: '', updated_at: null} for fresh users; (2) DELETE /api/chat/notes clears notes and returns {message: 'Session notes cleared'}; (3) After 6 POST /api/chat messages, asyncio.create_task fires update_session_notes() which calls OpenRouter and persists notes to DB — wait 8s after 6th message to verify; (4) GET /api/auth/me wraps user data in {"user": {...}} key — keep this in mind for frontend tests; (5) test@test.com has free_messages_count=12, minutes_left=3, test_used=False as of this test run.

endpoints_tested.load_personal_context smoke test: PASS — chat with notes in DB returns 200
